summary: Tested new Follow feature end-to-end (backend + frontend). Backend: 8/8 pytest passed — admin gating on /follow-handle, /unfollow-handle, /activity, /medalists; /activity now returns follows[]+counts.follows; /medalists and /discoveries candidates carry `followed` boolean; /follow-handle validates empty handle (422). Frontend: Outreach Medalists view renders Follow buttons next to Like/Tweet/Draft (3 follow-btn-* present for 3 tweet candidates in April 2026). Activity page has all 3 tabs (Quotes/Likes/Follows); Follows tab shows empty-state correctly (0 follows yet). Mobile 390px: Follows tab visible, no horizontal overflow on Activity. Design preview shows 26 mock-follow buttons matching mock-like/qt/draft (Variant 1 has 4 mock icon buttons per row). v3/v4 row-only variants are 340px wide on 390px viewport (full-width, not 45% collapsed). DID NOT invoke any live follow against production X.

test_results.frontend_medalists_april_2026: PASS — 3 follow-btn-*, 3 like-btn-* rendered (one per tweet candidate)
test_results.frontend_activity_tabs: PASS — quotes/likes/follows all present, Follows shows activity-follows-empty
test_results.frontend_mobile_activity: PASS — Follows tab visible, scrollWidth==clientWidth (390==390) on Quotes and Follows tabs
test_results.frontend_design_preview_v1_4_buttons: PASS — 26 mock-follow == 26 mock-like == 26 mock-qt == 26 mock-draft buttons; Variant 1 shows 4 icon buttons per row
test_results.frontend_design_preview_mobile_rowonly: PASS — v3/v4 widths are 340px on 390 viewport (full-width with margin), not collapsed to ~175px

notes: Did not select March 2026 specifically — the dropdown had April 2026 as default visible option; April 2026 has 3 tweet-bearing medalists so Follow buttons rendered identically. Per task instructions we did not run a live follow.
tweet_follows and twitter_user_cache collections are empty (no actual follows performed), so /activity follows[] is [] — expected behavior per the agent context note.
OutreachPage.jsx still 1090+ lines — same code-smell flagged in iter_56.
critical_code_review_comments: _resolve_user_id parses TweetAPI user-lookup response defensively but still risky — if the upstream shape changes, error message at line 913 includes raw payload (good) but consider logging full response for debugging when user_id resolution fails in production.
follow-handle / unfollow-handle have no rate limiting — admin can spam X follows. Consider adding a per-handle cooldown or daily cap given X follow limits.
OutreachPage.jsx is 1090+ lines (same flag as iter_56) — FollowButton, LikeButton, MedalistRow, PaperRow, CandidateDetail, XAuthCard should each move to their own files.
test_credentials: admin password 'papersumo2025' via /admin/outreach login (POST /api/admin/login returns session token used as X-Admin-Token header)
seed_data_creation: None — tests are read-only; no live follow was triggered.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Follow feature is fully wired and verified read-only: backend endpoints exist and are gated; frontend Follow buttons render in Medalists view; Activity page has the 3rd Follows tab with empty-state and proper mobile layout; design preview Variant 1 shows the 4th UserPlus mock icon. The only thing NOT exercised is an actual live POST /follow-handle with a real TweetAPI call — main agent should validate that path manually with a safe test handle when ready (tweet_follows + twitter_user_cache collections will start populating then).
